title: Smoky Masarepa Stir-fry
description: A smoky and dense vegan-friendly Kenyan dish featuring yellow masarepa mixed with toasted cashews, onions, and peppers.

ingredients.name: 3 bell peppers (red and green), thinly sliced
1 cup cashews, soaked overnight and toasted
3 tbsp coconut aminos or soy sauce

instructions: Toast the cashews in a dry skillet for about 2 minutes or until lightly golden. Set aside.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, add olive oil and sauté onion until translucent, about 4-5 minutes.
Add bell peppers and garlic to the skillet with some salt, cook for another 3-4 minutes until softened slightly.
Reduce heat to medium and add yellow masarepa into the pan. Stir constantly for about 2-3 minutes until the edges start to brown.
Pour in lime juice, coconut aminos or soy sauce, smoked paprika, and cashews. Mix well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Add fresh cilantro if using and stir again. Adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
Serve hot with steamed vegetables or rice.
